pattern: Once every recorded time is bucketed by dividing by a chunk size, counting tweets per chunk in a window is just a histogram over the (few) buckets that window spans.
hints: Store every recorded time for a given name in a simple list; recordTweet just appends to that name's list.

A frequency name picks a chunk width in seconds (minute=60, hour=3600, day=86400); the number of chunks covering [startTime, endTime] is ((endTime - startTime) // width) + 1, and any recorded time t falls in chunk (t - startTime) // width.
getTweetCountsPerFrequency builds a zero-filled array of that many chunks, then walks the name's recorded times, adding 1 to the right chunk for every time that falls within [startTime, endTime].

edge_inputs.description: a handful of tweets split across minute chunks, then re-queried by the hour
two different names never interfere with each other's counts
a window of exactly one second still yields exactly one chunk
day-frequency window spans multiple days and the last (partial) day chunk must still be counted
a tweet time exactly on a chunk boundary belongs to the chunk that starts at it, not the one before it
querying a window that starts after some recorded tweets must exclude those earlier tweets entirely, not shift the chunk index by them

stress.description: random recordTweet/getTweetCountsPerFrequency calls over a small name alphabet and bounded time range
stress.note: a bounded time range keeps chunk counts small for every frequency while still exercising all three chunk widths

complexity.time: O(k) per query, k = recorded tweets for that name
